EF1 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED WIND SPEEDS OF 100 MPH HAD A PATH WIDTH OF 250 YARDS AND A PATH LENGTH OF 11 MILES WHEN IT TOUCHED DOWN IN VERONA. SEVERAL HOMES AND BUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED. SEVERAL SHEDS AND A BARN WERE DESTROYED WITH SIGNS AND FENCING TAKEN OUT. THE TORNADO RESULTED IN A CHAOTIC AND CHOPPED UP PATTERN ALONG ITS LONG AND NARROW PATH.

A LINE OF THUNDERSTORMS PRODUCED A MACROBURST THAT EXTENDED FROM THE TOWN OF HAMDEN NEW YORK EAST TO A FEW MILES SOUTHEAST OF DELHI. THE DAMAGE FIRST APPEARED ON MUNN ROAD IN HAMDEN AND CONTINUED EAST FOR ABOUT 9 MILES CLOSE TO ROUTE 28 SOUTH OF DELHI WHERE IT ENDED ON GLEN BURNIE ROAD. THE DAMAGE WAS CONFINED TO TREES WHERE IN A FEW LOCATIONS THERE WERE NUMEROUS TREES UPROOTED AND/OR SNAPPED. MOST OF THE TREE DAMAGE WAS TO SOFTWOODS LIKE PINES...SPRUCE...CHERRY AND RED MAPLE.

DOWNBURST WIND DAMAGE FROM A THUNDERSTORM EXTENDED FROM NEAR EARLVILLE NY TO POOLVILLE NY. THE MAJORITY OF THE DAMAGE WAS TO TREES...WITH MOST TREES BEING UPROOTED. THERE WERE A FEW AREAS WHERE TREES WERE SNAPPED. THE CORE OF THE DAMAGE WHERE WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 90 MPH WAS TO A FARM WHERE A BARN WAAS BLOWN OFF ITS FOUNDATION BY A FEW INCHES. A TIN ROOF WAS PARTIALLY PEELED OFF AND SEVERAL LARGE HARDWOOD TREES WERE UPROOTED.

AN EF-0 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS OF 70 MPH WAS CONFIRMED NEAR THIS LOCATION. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN A RURAL AREA ALONG STATE ROUTE 4041 IN FAIRMOUNT TOWNSHIP. THE TORNADO DESTROYED A STORAGE SHED...GARDEN AND NUMEROUS TREES ALONG THIS ROAD BEFORE MOVING THROUGH A WOODED AREA AND STRIKING A DAIRY FARM ON RIDGE ROAD. A ROOF WAS TORN OFF A BARN...AND A GRAIN SILO WAS SHIFTED OFF IT/S FOUNDATION. THE TORNADO CROSSED OVER CORN AND HAY FIELDS BEFORE LIFTING ABOVE A NARROW VALLEY.

*** 4 FATAL *** TORNADO IN SMITHFIELD NORTHEAST OF PETERBORO. AN EF2 TORNADO PRODUCED 4 FATALITIES IN SMITHFIELD. PATH WIDTH WAS 235 YARDS. LENGTH WAS 2.5 MILES. 3 HOMES DESTROYED AND 5 ADDITIONAL HOMES SUSTAINED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE.

EF1 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS OF 90 MPH...PATH WIDTH OF 150 AND PATH LENGTH OF 1.4 MILES TOUCHED DOWN JUST SOUTH OF THE BOROUGH OF KINGSLEY. NUMEROUS LARGE TREES AND POWER LINES WERE KNOCKED DOWN WITH SEVERAL HOMES BEING STRUCK BY LARGE TREES. CHIMNEY PARTIALLY DAMAGED ON ONE HOME.

DOWNBURST WINDS IMPACTED A 10 MILE LONG AREA BY ABOUT 1 MILE WIDE. MOST OF THE DAMAGE WAS SPORADIC UNTIL NEAR ROUTE 365 AND FAIRCHILD ROAD. JUST SOUTH OF THE STOP AND GO ON ROUTE 365... HUNDREDS OF HARDWOOD TREES WERE UPROOTED AND SNAPPED. SOME MINOR DAMAGE TO A HOUSE WAS FOUND. STRONGEST WINDS WERE FOUND HERE AND ESTIMATED TO BE BETWEEN 85 AND 95 MPH. THE DAMAGE PATH CONTINUED NORTHEAST ACROSS ROUTE 365. TWO MORE SEPARATE BLOW DOWNS WERE FOUND NEAR TWIN ROCK ROAD. WINDS HERE WERE BETWEEN 75 AND

A SMALL TORNADO TRACKED FOR APPROXIMATELY 1.3 MILES THROUGH A RESIDENTIAL AREA NEAR UTICA NY. THE TORNADO WAS RATED AS EF-1 WITH MAXIMUM ESTIMATED WIND SPEEDS OF 90 MPH. DOZENS OF TREES WERE CHOPPED UP OR FELLED BY THE TORNADO... AND ONE RESIDENCE LOST TWO WALLS OF AN ATTACHED GARAGE. SEVERAL OTHER RESIDENCES EXPERIENCED SHINGLE AND SIDING DAMAGES.

DOWNBURST WINDS AFFECTED APPROXIMATELY A 2 MILE LONG...AND 1 MILE WIDE PATH OVER PORTIONS OF EXETER AND FRANKLIN TOWNSHIPS. SCATTERED TREE DAMAGE OCCURRED AT SEVERAL RESIDENCES...A CEMETARY AND DENSELY WOODED AREAS. ON LOCATION ALONG BODLE HILL ROAD HAD A CARPORT DESTROYED...AND A SMALL PATH OF TREE DAMAGE RUNNING DOWN A STEEP RIDGE LINE TO LEWIS ROAD.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR ALTAY ROAD AT 434 PM EDT IN THE TOWN OF READING AND TRACKED EAST SNAPPING AND UPROOTING MANY TREES ALONG ITS PATH. THE TORNADO BLEW A CANOE 150 YARDS FROM A POND AND WRAPPED IT AROUND A POLE. THERE WERE NUMEROUS LARGE SPLINTERS FROM TREES AND BRANCHES IMPALED IN THE GROUND. THE TORNADO PARTIALLY LIFTED A ROOF OFF OF A HOUSE AS IT TRACKED INTO THE WOODS. THE HOME WAS SEVERELY DAMAGED. THE TORNADO CONTINUED EAST CAUSING ONLY TREE DAMAGE BEFORE IT LIFTED BY ROUTE 14.

EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST EAST OF ELSTON HOLLOW ROAD WITH A PATH LENGTH OF 6 MILES AND MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF 150 YARDS. THIS TORNADO PRODUCED 100 MPH WINDS AND SNAPPED AND UPROOTED NUMEROUS TREES ALONG ITS PATH. A BARN WAS COMPLETELY COLLAPSED AND ONE BARN HAD ITS ROOF BLOWN OFF. A PICKUP TRUCK WAS LIFTED AND DROPPED BETWEEN 6 TO 12 FEET AWAY. A TRAILER ATTACHED TO THE TRUCK WAS DRAGGED UP TO 12 FEET AWAY. SEVERAL HOMES WERE ALSO DAMAGED.
